title: Mycoplasma Detection and Elimination Protocol for Cell Culture
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3c494","text":"Mycoplasma contamination affects up to 35% of cell cultures worldwide and is a serious threat to research integrity. This protocol provides a step-by-step approach to detecting and eliminating mycoplasma contamination using PurMa Biologics detection and elimination reagents, which are specifically designed for mammalian cell culture. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"5s2l","text":"- PurMa Biologics Mycoplasma Detection Kit","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3m1q5","text":"- PurMa Biologics Mycoplasma Elimination Reagent","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2iu76","text":"- PurMa Biologics Cell Culture Media","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bqpad","text":"- PurMa Biologics PBS Buffer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7haca","text":"- Cell culture flasks (T-25 or T-75)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bgdhe","text":"- Incubator (37°C, 5% CO2)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"33h59","text":"- Sterile pipettes and tips","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"61dj2","text":"- Centrifuge","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aek5p","text":"- Microcentrifuge tubes (1.5 mL)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Collect 1 mL of cell culture supernatant from your flask and transfer to a sterile 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.
Apply the PurMa Biologics Mycoplasma Detection Kit reagent to the sample according to kit instructions. Incubate for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Observe results. A positive result indicates mycoplasma contamination. Record findings before proceeding.
If positive, add PurMa Biologics Mycoplasma Elimination Reagent to the culture medium at the recommended concentration. Treat for 2 passages (approximately 7 days).
Re-test using the detection kit to confirm elimination. If still positive, repeat treatment for an additional 2 passages.
creator: Keon Azadi
